item.title: Confrontation between authorities and alleged correspondents' dinner shooter lasted 7 seconds
item.description: Seven seconds passed between when the alleged gunman at Saturday's White House Correspondents' Dinner — carrying a shotgun initially concealed by a jacket — first encountered federal law enforcement and when he was subdued, sources told CBS News.

item.title: Elon Musk concludes testimony in OpenAI trial
item.description: Elon Musk's testimony concluded Thursday in his lawsuit against OpenAI. Nicholas Thompson, CEO of The Atlantic and host of "The Most Interesting Thing in AI" podcast, joins "The Takeout" with analysis.

item.title: Florida GOP chair says voters will get "fair elections" due to new congressional map
item.description: Florida is moving forward with Gov. Ron DeSantis' plan to redraw its congressional map that would likely give Republicans more seats. Evan Power, chair of the Florida Republican Party, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Iran war's true cost closer to $50 billion, not $25 billion, U.S. officials say
item.description: A Pentagon official publicly placed the Department of Defense's cost for Operation Epic Fury at $25 billion.

item.title: True cost of Iran war closer to $50 billion, not $25 billion, U.S. officials say
item.description: The true cost of the war with Iran is closer to $50 billion, not $25 billion, according to U.S. officials familiar with internal assessments. CBS News' Ramy Inocencio and Zak Hudak have more.

item.title: Iran war pushes oil prices to 4-year high as Hegseth faces lawmakers
item.description: Oil prices hit a 4-year high as Axios reports Trump will hear new options to try to break the Strait of Hormuz standoff with Iran with a new wave of attacks.

item.title: Texas mother of 4 released from ICE custody after more than a month in detention
item.description: Meenu Batra, a single mother of four adult U.S. citizens, was arrested on March 17 by federal immigration officers while traveling for a work trip.

item.title: New Mexico Gov. Lujan Grisham explains how the state's universal childcare program works
item.description: New Mexico is facing a challenge to its free universal childcare program, the only one of its kind in the U.S. Democratic Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham joins "The Takeout" to break down how it works.

item.title: Ringleader of $250M fraud scheme leaked protected documents from jail, prosecutors say
item.description: The United States Attorney's Office filed a motion on Tuesday that says Aimee Bock, since at least February, has been directing her college-age son to "download large volumes of material related to her federal prosecution," and disseminate them to lawmakers and members of the media.

item.title: Trump signs bill funding DHS, ending record-breaking 76-day shutdown
item.description: The longest shutdown of a federal department in U.S. history came to an end on Thursday when President Trump signed a bill to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security following a breakthrough on Capitol Hill.

item.title: Trump officials flew on 1st non-stop commercial flight from US to Venezuela in years
item.description: The trip comes nearly four months after U.S. forces seized Rodríguez's predecessor, Nicolás Maduro​, and his wife in a daring special forces raid.

item.title: Ex-federal official worried U.S. "may not come out on top" if Iran war becomes "battle of endurance"
item.description: The ceasefire with Iran is holding, but negotiations are stalled. The ripple effects: tight energy supplies, price spikes and recession risks. Edward Fishman, former Russian and Europe sanctions lead at the State Department, joins with analysis.

item.title: "Ozempic breath" is boosting Hershey's sales of mints and gum
item.description: Hershey says it's benefiting from the growing use of GLP-1 weight-loss drugs even as people cut down on snacks. Here's why.

item.title: Details revealed on where Cole Allen is being held in D.C. jail complex
item.description: Cole Allen, 31, is charged with attempting to assassinate President Trump and two firearms-related offenses stemming from the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner on Saturday night.

item.title: D4vd killed Celeste Rivas Hernandez to "silence" her, prosecutors allege
item.description: The singer is accused of killing 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez.

item.title: Watch: Sen. Gary Peters presses Hegseth on the need to reopen of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Democratic Sen. Gary Peters told Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth the U.S. needs to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. "We're not going to bring this war to an end until we seize control of the Strait in a way that opens it back up," Peters said. Hegseth responded by pointing to the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports.

item.title: Video shows shocking moment of Queens explosion, sending NYPD officers to hospital
item.description: NYPD body camera footage captures a Queens explosion early Thursday morning that triggered a fire, filling the air with smoke. One person was killed and multiple NYPD officers were sent to the hospital, officials said.

item.title: Lawyer for Texas mother who spent over a month in ICE detention says "this has broken her"
item.description: A single mother of four is now free after ICE held her in a Texas immigration detention center for more than a month. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has the latest.

item.title: Trump drops Casey Means as surgeon general nominee, names Nicole Saphier
item.description: President Trump picked Dr. Nicole Saphier as his new nominee for surgeon general, and blamed Republican Sen. Bill Cassidy for Casey Mean's nomination stalling in the Senate.

item.title: What could come next in U.S. war with Iran
item.description: American drivers are paying $1.32 more per gallon than they did before the Iran war. Hopes of a quick end continue to fade as U.S. and Iranian leaders remain defiant. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak has more on what could come next.

item.title: Rep. James Walkinshaw previews line of questioning for Bondi's Epstein deposition
item.description: Pam Bondi is set to testify late May on the Justice Department's handling of the Jeffrey Epstein files in a closed-door deposition before the House Oversight Committee. Democratic Rep. James Walkinshaw of Virginia joins "The Takeout" to discuss what he wants to hear from the former attorney general.

item.title: Watch: Hegseth accuses Slotkin of asking "gotcha hypothetical" question in tense exchange
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth shot back at Democratic Sen. Elissa Slotkin when she asked whether he would carry out President Trump's order to seize ballots and voting machines during the 2026 midterm elections if the president directed him to.

item.title: How the 76-day Homeland Security shutdown started, and how it ended
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security funding shutdown ended on Thursday after 76 days. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion has the details.

item.title: Elon Musk clashes with OpenAI attorney during cross-examination at trial
item.description: Elon Musk was cross-examined in his lawsuit against OpenAI on Thursday. In testimony on Wednesday, he said he was "a fool" for funding OpenAI. He is accusing OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman, of betraying him and the public by abandoning its core mission as it transitioned from a nonprofit to a for-profit company. WIRED senior writer Maxwell Zeff joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: What states could try to redistrict and add more GOP seats
item.description: The Supreme Court's ruling on Wednesday about Louisiana's congressional map could have implications for several states as it narrowed the section of the Voting Rights Act about majority-minority districts.

item.title: How "The Hills" star Spencer Pratt became a serious contender in LA mayoral race
item.description: Spencer Pratt, who starred in the mid-2000s reality TV show "The Hills," released a new ad in his campaign for Los Angeles mayor that's getting attention online. CBS News political director Fin Gómez joins with analysis.

item.title: Congress passes another short-term FISA extension, hours before deadline
item.description: The House and Senate on Thursday passed an extension of a key surveillance authority that allows U.S. intelligence agencies to spy on foreigners without a warrant, the latest in a back-and-forth over the expiring program.

item.title: Mark Kelly slams Pete Hegseth at hearing: "The things you say matters"
item.description: Democratic Sen. Mark Kelly criticized Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth for his answers to questions during a hearing before the Senate Armed Services Committee.

item.title: Spotify's new badge identifies human artists as AI music floods include
item.description: As AI-generated music spreads, Spotify says it wants to help users "trust the authenticity" of what they're listening to.

item.title: Thermos recalls 8.2 million containers after stoppers caused vision loss
item.description: The Thermos Stainless King Food Jars and Thermos Sportsman Food & Beverage Bottles were sold at Walmart, Target and Amazon.com.
